HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Mehran Nazar, DDS' Further Discovery Responses.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications, as follows:

The Motion is denied as to Special Interrogatory ("SI") No.'s 12 & 87, as plaintiff answered these interrogatories prior to the hearing making this aspect moot.
The Motion is denied as to SI No.'s 89, 91, 93, 100 & 104 as plaintiff reasonably identified the responsive document(s), and as defendant's instructions in the SI was improper.
The Motion is denied as to SI No. 99 as the interrogatory is vague, ambiguous and overbroad, and as plaintiff did provide an answer.
The Motion is granted in part as to SI No. 105 & 106 to cover the time period of 2014 to the present, and for SI No. 106 as to actual membership agreements.

The Motion is denied as to Request for Production of Documents ("RFPD") No.'s 1 & 26 as the requests are overbroad, overly broad in time and scope, and each category is not reasonably particularized. Motion is granted as to RFPD No. 22 to conform with the court's ruling as to SI No. 105 & 106 to cover the time period of 2014 to the present.

The Motion is denied as to RFPD No.'s 6, 32, 33, 35, 36, 38, 43, 44, 45, 46, 56,58 & 59 as plaintiff's response did confirm a good faith and diligent search for responsive documents.
The Motion is granted as to RFPD No.'s 7 & 57 as plaintiff's response is not code compliant

The moving party is to prepare a proposed order in editable MS Word format and e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at maulawsf@hotmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Michael Mau; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

MOTION / ** Redacted ** Notice Of Motion & Motion To Retain Confidentiality Of Anti-Fraud Detection Proceduremanual; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Nov-04-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Retain Confidentiality Of Anti-Fraud & Detection Procedure Manual.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

As an initial matter, the scope of the Motion is limited to the designation of the limited portions of the Anti-Fraud & Detection Procedure Manual (Manual) that are being challenged by Plaintiff (contained on pages 19, 32, and 33 (Opp., ft. 3)). No challenge has been made to the designation of the other portions of the Manual. Therefore, in this Motion, no ruling is made on the designation of the other parts of the Manual. (See Protective Order ("PO"), Shih Decl., Ex. F, Section 6). The Protective Order by its own language allows a challenge to designated material "at any time" Id.

Second, the Court makes a distinction between analyzing (1) whether to retain a confidential designation per the PO, and (2) whether to seal documents for filing per California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court notes that the August 3, 2020 ex parte application requested that the Court seal documents that were already filed with the Court. Judge Schulman's order on the ex parte changed the words "seal" to "withdraw" and "withdraw from the docket." (Shih Decl., Ex. F). Similarly, the PO in this case makes a distinction between challenging a designation and filing a designated document under the Rules of Court. (Shih Decl, Ex. A, Sections 6, 10).

The material at issue in this Motion were designated "Highly Confidential" pursuant to PO, 2.6. That Section defines Highly Confidential information as "information (regardless of how generated, stored or maintained) including pricing, business practices or decisions or other business information that is so competitively sensitive that the Designating Party reasonably and in good faith concludes that disclosure of such information would create a substantial risk of serious injury." Based upon this definition and the evidence provided by Plaintiff, the Court rules as follows.

The Motion is GRANTED as to the portions challenged by Plaintiff. The Court finds that the portions of the Manual are not identical to the information on the website, the fact that the definitions are similar to commonly understood definitions is not dispositive, Defendant has shown sufficient interest to retain confidentiality, and Defendant has shown prejudice if the designation is removed. Furthermore, Plaintiffs have not shown how the designation interferes with their ability to litigate this case. The Court makes no ruling on whether or not the portions of the Manual challenged in this Motion are admissible at trial.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at peter.catalanotti@wilsonelser.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported (302/JPT)

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-08-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs KHEHRA DENTAL CORPORATION, HARJIT KHEHRA D.D.S.'s DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT:

Cross-defendant Khehra's demurrer to the breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing cause of action is overruled. Paragraphs 8-9 of the cross-complaint allege that the MAPP Rules and PDRs are incorporated into the PDA. Violation of those rules appears to be the basis for the breach of contract and breach of implied covenant claims. While the claims appear to overlap to some degree, the court determines that this issue would be better addressed on summary judgment/adjudication because a factual issue remains regarding whether the adduced rules were actually incorporated into the PDA. (See Blickman Turkus, LP v. MF Downtown Sunnyvale, LLC (2008) 162 Cal.App.4th 858, 890 [redundancy defect not basis for demurrer and ordinarily addressed through summary judgment motion]; but see Palm Springs Villas II Homeowners Assn., Inc. v. Parth (2016) 248 Cal.App.4th 268, 290 [sustaining demurrer to duplicative cause of action].)

Cross-Defendant Harjit Khehra, D.D.S.?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Cross-Complainant?S Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-08-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs/Cross-Defendants KHEHRA DENTAL CORPORATION and HARJIT KHEHRA D.D.S' Motion To Strike Portions Of Cross-Complainant'S Cross-Complaint:

Cross-defendant Khehra's motion to strike is granted without leave to amend.

Delta Dental cannot recover attorney's fees as damages. The general rule is that a party pays its own attorney's fees. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1021; Mountain Air Enterprises, LLC v. Sundowner Towers, LLC (2017) 3 Cal.5th 744, 751 ["Under the American rule, each party to a lawsuit ordinarily pays its own attorney fees."]; La Mirada Avenue Neighborhood Assn. of Hollywood v. City of Los Angeles (2018) 22 Cal.App.5th 149, 1155.) The cross-complaint does not allege that any of the relevant agreements contained a prevailing party attorney's fees clause. Absent such an express fees clause in an underlying agreement, attorney's fees expended in litigation are not recoverable as damages for breach of the agreement. (Navellier v. Sletten (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 763, 776 ["Plaintiffs' major item of damages, the attorney's fees they incurred in connection with defendant's counterclaims, is not available as a matter of law because neither a statute nor the release provides for recovery of attorney's fees in this case."]; Olson v. Arnett (1980) 113 Cal.App.3d 59, 67-68 ["The instant case is based on a contract, the agreement to settle the underlying action. There is no contention or evidence there was any provision in the contract for attorney's fees. Appellant breached his contract, and respondents had to employ attorneys in order to enforce that contract. We think this case is not basically different from any other contract action where the nonbreaching party is forced to employ an attorney to enforce the contract but is not entitled to his attorney's fees as damages."].)

Corporations Code sec. 7341(g) is not a separate basis to recover fees. Under that provision, Delta can only recover "fees incurred before the termination." In this case, Delta is seeking post-termination litigation fees.

Delta Dental fails to plead facts showing that the "tort of another" doctrine applies. Delta Dental's cited federal antitrust cases are inapposite.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Motion To Stay
Off Calendar Dec-19-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Andrew Goldman, Esq. 415-435-5500 of the Goldman Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Howard Ullman, Esq. 415-773-5652 of Orrick Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues 3 points. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Stay.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Delta Dental's motion to stay is denied. The Court does not disagree that the Membership Action Committee's decision is reviewable under the common law doctrine of fair procedure and the MAPP Rules by way of administrative mandamus in Superior Court. (See, e.g., Delta Dental Plan of California v. Banasky (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 1598, 1605-1608.) However, Defendant has not conclusively established that such a claim is the only cause of action that Dr. Khehra may bring following the termination of her membership or contract, or that all of her causes of action solely constitute challenges to such termination. (See, e.g., Economy v. Sutter East Bay Hospitals (2019) 31 Cal.App.5th 1147, 1155 & fn. 6 [physician whose hospital privileges were suspended brought causes of action against hospital for violations of statutory and common law right to fair procedure, aiding and abetting, conspiracy, intentional interference with prospective economic advantage, and interference with right to practice profession, some of which were resolved against him on summary judgment prior to trial].) Further, Plaintiffs have not filed a petition for writ of mandate seeking such review and have disclaimed any intention to do so, and accordingly there is no pending mandate proceeding to which the Court should defer. Under the circumstances, the Court does not find that a stay of the action is warranted or is the appropriate procedural vehicle by which to address Defendant's defenses.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
